docs.title: Welcome to Cars: Mater-National Modding!
docs.text:
This site serves as a central database for all things Cars: Mater-National modding; from documentation of the game's C++ classes to tutorials on how to make custom animations!

As this site is currently under construction, most pages are empty, and significant amount of information is missing. If you're looking to help add new pages or revise existing ones, feel free to contribute via PR!

docs.title: as
docs.text: Stands for ActionScript, a proprietary ParameterBlock-based scripting language used for story events. (Not to be confused with Adobe Flash's scripting language!)

docs.title: aty
docs.text: ParameterBlock files that describe activity information.

docs.title: aud
docs.text: ParameterBlock files that describe the contents of sound banks.

docs.title: bik
docs.text: Videos encoded in the Bink Video format, part of the RAD Game Tools middleware.

docs.title: bxml
docs.text: Stands for Binary XML. Used in Mater-National to describe keyframe animations for UI elements.

docs.title: cel
docs.text: A proprietary format that stores font cell information.

docs.title: cmt
docs.text: Stands for Collision Material Table. These are ParameterBlock files that map ground collision materials to sounds.

docs.title: cwd
docs.text: Stands for Crowd.

docs.title: dds
docs.text: Microsoft DirectDraw Surface textures. Can be viewed and edited with most image manipulation tools such as GIMP, Paint.NET, and Adobe Photoshop.

docs.title: hlsl
docs.text: High-Level-Shader-Language (HLSL) Source Code.

docs.title: prt
docs.text: ParameterBlock files that store particle effect information.

docs.title: psh
docs.text: Compiled HLSL Pixel Shader.

docs.title: res
docs.text: Stands for Resource File. Used to package files often loaded and unloaded at the same time together.

docs.title: scn
docs.text: ParameterBlock files that describe the contents of a scene. (placement of static models, collision objects, lighting information, etc.)

docs.title: soi
docs.text: Stands for Streaming Object Information. Stores general information about a streaming container, as well as every header for every element/file in the container.

docs.title: spl
docs.text: ParameterBlock files that store spline curves. Usage unknown (likely AI?)

docs.title: slt
docs.text: ParameterBlock files that describe renderable models in plaintext, similar to OBJ. This format is not usable in any of the Rainbow Studios games by default, so any files must be converted to GCG/PSG/DXG/P3G/XNG

docs.title: str
docs.text: Streaming data heap, must be paired with a SOI and TOC to form a streaming container.

docs.title: sty
docs.text: ParameterBlock files that describe a UI style sheet.

docs.title: tga
docs.text: Truevision TGA textures. Can be viewed and edited with most image manipulation tools such as GIMP, Paint.NET, and Adobe Photoshop.

docs.title: toc
docs.text: Maps streaming component IDs with instance IDs, and stores information about the layout and contents of the adjacent STR file.

docs.title: toy
docs.text: ParameterBlock files that describe the placement of Toy objects within scenes or collections.

docs.title: trk
docs.text: ParameterBlock files that store track information.

docs.title: vsh
docs.text: Compiled HLSL Vertex Shader.

docs.title: xng
docs.text: Stands for Xbox 360 Geometry. This format describes renderable models, similar to OBJ and FBX. Used in the Xbox 360, Playstation 3, and PC versions of Mater-National.

docs.title: xnm
docs.text: Stands for Xbox 360 Material. These ParameterBlock-based files describe individual materials by what shader they use, which textures should be bound and to which registers, and much more.
